linting.notes: kube-linter currently reports 240 lint violations across manifests. These are policy violations (missing resource limits, security contexts, etc.) not syntax errors. Kustomize builds succeed with deprecation warnings about 'commonLabels'.

testing.framework: Bash integration tests using github.com/opendatahub-io/peak test runner
setup_required: Requires active OpenShift cluster with admin access
env_vars: KUBECONFIG - path to OpenShift cluster config
SKIP_INSTALL=true - skip installation, test existing deployment
TESTS_REGEX=<pattern> - filter which tests to run
OPENSHIFT_TESTUSER_NAME - test user for Dashboard UI tests
ARTIFACT_DIR=/tmp/artifacts - output directory for test artifacts
testing.mock_patterns: Tests deploy real components to cluster, no mocking
testing.notes: Tests are integration tests that require a real OpenShift cluster. They cannot run in a standard container without cluster access. Tests install ODH components, wait for pods to be ready, and verify functionality. Typical test run: 30+ minutes. Test image built from tests/Dockerfile includes oc CLI, selenium, chromium for Dashboard UI tests.

config_files: External: openshift/release repo contains job definitions

gating_checks.name: odh-manifests-pr-test
gating_checks.command: Runs in OpenShift CI: podman build test image, deploy to ephemeral cluster, run tests/basictests/*

ci.notes: CI configuration is external to this repo, maintained in openshift/release repository. Tests run in ephemeral OpenShift clusters provisioned in AWS via ipi-aws workflow. No GitHub Actions workflows. CI job definition at: https://github.com/openshift/release/blob/master/ci-operator/config/opendatahub-io/odh-manifests/

build.notes: This is a Kustomize manifests repository - no code compilation. 'Building' means rendering Kustomize overlays into final YAML. Each component has base/ and optional overlays/. Use kube-linter to validate manifests, kustomize to validate composition.

conventions.test_naming: Bash scripts with .sh extension, named after component (dashboard.sh, dsp-operator.sh, etc.)
conventions.notes: Kustomize structure: each component has base/kustomization.yaml. Tests use os::cmd:: functions from peak test framework. YAML files follow Kubernetes resource conventions.

lint_commands.notes: Exit code 1, found 240 lint violations (policy issues, not syntax errors)
Exit code 0, builds successfully with deprecation warnings

test_commands.notes: Requires OpenShift cluster - cannot validate without infrastructure

container_recipe.notes: Linting can be validated in container. Tests require OpenShift cluster with admin credentials and cannot run in standard container. For test validation, use OpenShift CI or provision test cluster.

results.command: dnf install -y wget tar gzip make
wget kube-linter && tar -xzf kube-linter-linux.tar.gz && mv kube-linter /usr/local/bin/
wget kustomize && tar -xzf kustomize_v5.3.0_linux_amd64.tar.gz && mv kustomize /usr/local/bin/

results.output_snippet:
Error: found 240 lint errors
latest-tag, no-read-only-root-fs, run-as-non-root, unset-cpu-requirements, unset-memory-requirements, dangling-service, non-existent-service-account
results.notes: Linter ran successfully and found policy violations. Exit code 1 indicates lint issues found (expected behavior). Issues are policy/best-practice violations, not syntax errors.

results.output_snippet:

apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: odh-dashboard
results.notes: Kustomize builds manifests successfully with deprecation warnings
Tests require OpenShift cluster access - cannot validate in isolated container

validation.summary: Linting validated successfully (kube-linter found 240 policy violations, exit code 1). Kustomize builds validated (successful with warnings). Tests cannot be validated without OpenShift cluster.

gaps: Tests require full OpenShift cluster with admin access - cannot run in CI without cluster provisioning
No unit tests - only integration tests
kube-linter reports 240 policy violations that should be addressed
Kustomize uses deprecated 'commonLabels' field in multiple components
CI configuration is external (openshift/release repo) - not visible in this repository
Tests take 30+ minutes to run (cluster provisioning + ODH installation + validation)
No way to validate Dashboard UI tests without running selenium in test container against real cluster
